task.title: Add external planning and memory adapter examples for task-packet handoffs.

context.problem_statement: Target repos need examples for linking backlog mirrors to Keryx, Obsidian, issues, or other planning systems without turning repo-contract-kit into a planning application.
background: AGW-059 is repo-contract-kit docs/template work over the existing backlog-status, backlog-check, agent-next, and task-packet surfaces.
Current Codex_CodeReview backlog status is 2 open, 99 done, 0 partial; AGW-059 is the next open item.
AGW-058 is committed in repo-contract-kit as d4a22f0 and dogfooded in Codex_CodeReview as 31859f1.
Current Codex_CodeReview and repo-contract-kit checkouts are clean before this packet is written.
non_goals: Do not add Keryx, Obsidian, GitHub Issues, Linear, Jira, or hosted planning API clients.
Do not make repo-contract-kit the source of priority truth for target repos.
Do not write to external memory systems, vault files, issues, pull requests, labels, or project boards.
Do not change task-packet schema semantics unless examples expose a strictly necessary documentation-only clarification.

previous_task_state.notes: AGW-059 may start after this packet commit; no active sibling tasks or dirty files were reported before packet creation.

closeout_required_before_start.reason: The prior task is committed in both repos, kit-status is current, and task-status reports no active tasks or coordination warnings.
closeout_required_before_start.required_next_step: Commit this AGW-059 packet, then implement only the scoped repo-contract-kit docs/examples/templates work.

goal_alignment.repo_goal: repo-contract-kit installs local-first docs-as-code and agent workflow guardrails while preserving target-owned planning decisions.

area_contracts.purpose: Maintainer and operator guidance for backlog mirrors, task packets, and external planning handoffs.
area_contracts.notes: Examples should explain adapter boundaries without adding runtime integrations.

area_contracts.purpose: Installed target-repo guidance where external planning handoff examples should travel with the kit.
area_contracts.notes: Only edit templates if the examples need to be installed into target repos.

area_contracts.purpose: Regression coverage for installed docs/templates and freshness checks.
area_contracts.notes: Add or adjust tests only if new installed files or command references require coverage.

stop_conditions: Stop if the implementation requires external service credentials or network calls.
Stop if examples would make repo-contract-kit own prioritization instead of mirroring selected external planning state.
Stop if the work becomes a Keryx, Obsidian, GitHub, Linear, or Jira adapter implementation rather than examples.

protected_files: External Keryx, Obsidian, GitHub, Linear, Jira, or planning-system state.
Credentials, tokens, browser sessions, vault files, issue trackers, PRs, labels, and project boards.
Task-packet schema behavior unless a documentation-only clarification is necessary.
expected_outputs: Docs/examples showing Keryx/Obsidian/issues/backlog mirrors as planning sources that feed one selected task packet.
Sample docs/backlog.md mirror patterns with explicit source-of-truth and sync boundaries.
Installed guidance if needed so target repos receive the planning-handoff examples.
Validation that examples do not add external writes, credentials, or planning-app behavior.
Version/changelog updates in repo-contract-kit, followed by dogfood and backlog closeout in Codex_CodeReview.

phase_files.reason: The work should be a bounded docs/examples slice with no external integration code.

phases.title: Planning Adapter Examples
phases.objective: Add and validate external planning/memory handoff examples, then dogfood and close AGW-059.
allowed_scope: Codex_CodeReview dogfood metadata and backlog closeout files
completion_evidence: Codex_CodeReview kit-status and backlog checks pass
AGW-059 is marked done in source and mirror backlog files
phases.handoff_notes: Split a separate task if implementation requires service-specific API clients or writable sync.

acceptance_criteria.description: Examples show how external planning or memory systems hand off one selected item into a task packet without becoming repo-contract-kit state.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Review docs/examples for explicit source-of-truth, mirror, and one-item task-packet boundaries.

acceptance_criteria.description: Keryx, Obsidian, issues, and docs/backlog.md mirror patterns are described as examples, not built-in adapters.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Confirm no new external API clients, credential handling, vault writes, or issue mutation code is added.

acceptance_criteria.description: Installed target guidance is updated where useful and remains local-first.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Install or inspect templates and run docs freshness/install tests if installed files change.

acceptance_criteria.description: Operator docs explain what to do when external planning state and repo backlog mirrors disagree.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Review guidance for stale mirror handling, human priority authority, and safe task-packet selection.

commands.expected_result: focused installed-doc and docs-freshness coverage passes if templates changed
repo-contract-kit VERSION and CHANGELOG are valid
Codex_CodeReview dogfood install updates safely from the local kit checkout

commands.command: make backlog-check && make backlog-split-check && make backlog-status && make agent-next
commands.expected_result: Codex_CodeReview backlog mirrors remain valid and AGW-059 closeout advances the next item
Codex_CodeReview reports the dogfooded kit version/source ref

commands.command: make docs-check && make version-check && git diff --check

evidence_to_capture: Focused and full repo-contract-kit validation output
Evidence that no external writes/API clients were added
Backlog closeout status and next open item

readiness_check.command: make agent-task-ready TASK=AGW-059 TASK_READY_JSON=1 or record why unavailable
readiness_check.expected_result: readiness passes or a blocker is recorded before handoff

lifecycle_action.command: make agent-task-finalize TASK=AGW-059 TASK_RECEIPT=.agent-workflows/tasks/AGW-059/receipt.json TASK_FINALIZE_JSON=1 or durable fallback receipt
lifecycle_action.expected_result: task metadata is terminal and final receipt is linked, or fallback receipt is documented

final_task_status.expected_result: terminal task state, receipt path, and active overlaps are visible or unavailable state is explained

closeout_preview.expected_result: closeout preview reports expected changes; applying closeout requires explicit approval

closeout_requirements.dirty_state_explanation: Final handoff must classify any remaining dirty files in both repo-contract-kit and Codex_CodeReview.

docs_impact.notes: This task is explicitly about docs/examples for planning handoffs.

known_risks: Examples could be mistaken for supported writable adapters.
Backlog mirrors could be treated as priority truth instead of reviewed copies from an external planner.
External memory examples could encourage unsafe vault or issue tracker writes.
Installed docs could duplicate or conflict with existing backlog command guidance.
stop_conditions: Stop if implementation requires external API credentials, network access, or writable sync.
Stop if examples cannot state a clear source-of-truth and stale-mirror policy.
Stop if target repo update produces conflicts that cannot be safely classified.

approval.notes: Approved by the user's serial backlog execution request.

token_budget.notes: No explicit token budget for the active goal.

dependencies: Commit this packet before editing repo-contract-kit.
Commit repo-contract-kit implementation before dogfood closeout in Codex_CodeReview.
handoff.next_packet_hint: AGW-060 should remain a separate guided-upgrade-flow task after AGW-059 is closed.
